module.name: probtree
module.description: A two-level probability tree: branches with probabilities, leaves showing the joint probability (auto-multiplied). Sections: each = one branch, "Label | p" lines.
module.aiSpec:
A PROBABILITY TREE (two levels, left→right) with the joint probability computed at each leaf. Use for conditional-probability / Bayes setups. Block module: split the body with `<!-- @ -->`, one FIRST-LEVEL branch per section: • the section's FIRST line is that branch: `Label | p` (p as 0.3 or 30%), • each following line is a second-level outcome under it: `Label | p`. Parameters: `root` (text, default "Start") — label of the start node. `joint` (boolean, default true) — false hides the auto-computed p1×p2 at leaves. `color` (color, default accent) — accent for nodes and connectors. `percent` (boolean, default false) — show probabilities as percentages (30%) instead of decimals (0.3). `precision` (number 0-6, default 4) — max decimal places when rounding probabilities. `gap` (number px 0-80, default 18) — vertical spacing between first-level branches.
<!-- @probtree root: Weather, percent: true --> Rain | 0.3 Late | 0.5 On time | 0.5 <!-- @ --> Sun | 0.7 Late | 0.1 On time | 0.9 <!-- @end -->

param.name: root
param.label: Root label
param.desc: Label of the start node on the left.

param.name: joint
param.label: Show joint probabilities
param.desc: Show the auto-computed p1×p2 value at each leaf.

param.name: color
param.label: Accent
param.desc: Accent color for nodes and connector lines.

param.name: percent
param.label: Show as percent
param.desc: Display probabilities as percentages (30%) instead of decimals (0.3).

param.name: precision
param.label: Decimal places
param.desc: Maximum decimal places when rounding probabilities.

snippet.label: Probability tree
snippet.text: <!-- @probtree root: Test --> Positive | 0.2 Disease | 0.9 Healthy | 0.1 <!-- @ --> Negative | 0.8 Disease | 0.05 Healthy | 0.95 <!-- @end -->
snippet.description: Two-level tree; joint probabilities auto-computed at leaves.
